title: Country Representative
summary: WorldFish is recruiting a Country Representative to provide country-level leadership for aquatic food systems research, partnerships and programme delivery.
description: The Country Representative role sits at the interface of research leadership, country strategy, partner engagement and programme implementation. The post is suited to a senior professional who can represent WorldFish with government, CGIAR, donor and implementation partners while helping translate aquatic food systems science into country-level impact.
verification_notes: Verified against the WorldFish careers page, which lists current vacancies and deadlines; the source page provides limited role detail.
responsibilities: Represent WorldFish at country level with government, donors, research partners and development actors.
Lead or coordinate country strategy, programme planning and implementation for aquatic food systems work.
Support resource mobilization, partnership development and delivery of high-quality project outputs.
Ensure country work contributes to nutrition, climate resilience, sustainability, gender and livelihoods outcomes.
requirements: Senior professional experience in aquatic food systems, agriculture, fisheries, development, research management or a related field.
Strong leadership, partnership-building and stakeholder engagement skills.
Experience managing programmes, teams or institutional relationships in an international development or research setting.
Ability to work across government, donor, community and research networks.
benefits: Compensation is not specified in the supplied record.
Leadership role with WorldFish, a CGIAR research institution focused on aquatic foods for healthy people, healthy planet and shared prosperity.

title: Business Operations Analyst – Africa
summary: CIMMYT, through CIFOR-ICRAF recruitment, is hiring a Business Operations Analyst for Africa to support finance, operations, reporting and business process improvement across regional programmes.
description: This role supports operational management for CIMMYT activities in Africa, linking programme delivery with finance, administration, compliance and data-driven reporting. It is suited to candidates who can turn operational information into reliable management support for country and regional teams.
verification_notes: Verified from the CIFOR-ICRAF/CIMMYT careers listing and public career-index references.
responsibilities: Analyze business operations, budgets, workflows and programme support needs for Africa-based activities.
Prepare management reports, dashboards, operational trackers and documentation for decision-making.
Coordinate with finance, administration, procurement and programme teams on compliance and delivery.
Identify process improvements that make regional operations more efficient and accountable.
requirements: Experience in business operations, finance, administration, project support or data analysis.
Strong spreadsheet, reporting and analytical skills.
Understanding of nonprofit, research, agriculture or international development operations is an advantage.
Ability to work across multicountry teams and communicate clearly with technical and operational colleagues.
benefits: Compensation is not specified in the supplied record.
Opportunity to support CIMMYT agricultural research and operational delivery across Africa.

title: Associate Scientist – Data Analyst
summary: ICRISAT, through CIFOR-ICRAF recruitment, is hiring an Associate Scientist - Data Analyst to support agricultural research with data management, analysis and evidence generation.
description: The role is for a scientist/data analyst who can manage and analyze research data, develop reproducible workflows, and support teams using data for agricultural, environmental or development research. It bridges statistical analysis, research support and communication of results.
verification_notes: Verified from the CIFOR-ICRAF/ICRISAT careers listing and public career-index references.
responsibilities: Clean, manage and analyze research datasets for agricultural and development projects.
Develop reproducible analysis workflows, visualizations and technical documentation.
Support scientists with study design, data quality checks, statistical analysis and interpretation.
Prepare tables, figures, briefs or reports that translate data into usable evidence.
requirements: Degree and experience in statistics, data science, agricultural science, economics, computer science or a related field.
Strong skills in data analysis tools such as R, Python, STATA, SPSS, SQL or similar platforms.
Experience handling research datasets and documenting analysis workflows.
Ability to work with multidisciplinary research teams and communicate findings clearly.
benefits: Compensation is not specified in the supplied record.
Opportunity to support ICRISAT agricultural research with data-driven analysis.

title: General Call for Applications – Consultant Roster
summary: CIFOR-ICRAF is maintaining a consultant roster for specialists who can support forestry, agroforestry, climate, value-chain, governance, gender, communications, legal, finance, IT and administrative assignments.
description: The roster is an expression-of-interest mechanism for future consultancy needs. CIFOR-ICRAF uses rosters to identify qualified experts who may be matched to assignments across research, policy, programme delivery and institutional support functions.

responsibilities: Register expertise and availability for future consultancy assignments.
Provide technical, advisory, research, communications, legal, finance, IT or operational services when matched to an assignment.
Deliver agreed outputs such as analysis, reports, tools, facilitation, training, documentation or project support.
Work with CIFOR-ICRAF teams and partners across diverse geographic and thematic contexts.
requirements: Relevant degree or professional background for the specialization submitted.
Demonstrated consultancy, research, advisory or institutional support experience.
Strong written and spoken English; additional languages may strengthen applications.
Ability to work in multicultural, multidisciplinary teams and deliver assignments to agreed timelines.
benefits: Competitive remuneration commensurate with skills and experience where an assignment is awarded.
Potential access to future consultancy assignments with CIFOR-ICRAF.

title: Education and Technology Researcher
organization: African Population and Health Research Center (APHRC)
summary: APHRC is recruiting an Education and Technology Researcher in Nairobi to lead and contribute to research on digital innovations, AI in education, and evidence-informed education policy in Africa.
description: The role is based in APHRC’s Human Development Theme and focuses on the intersection of education, technology and digital innovation. The researcher will generate evidence on responsible use and scaling of digital technologies and AI in African education systems, with occasional travel to project sites in sub-Saharan Africa.
source: African Population and Health Research Center (APHRC)
verification_notes: Verified from the APHRC official career page.
responsibilities: Lead or co-lead research studies and evaluations on digital technology and AI in African basic education.
Strengthen APHRC’s knowledge base on education technology, equity, ethics and scalable digital learning models.
Develop proposals, secure funding and manage research resources for education and technology work.
Mentor younger researchers and translate findings into policy briefs, research reports and peer-reviewed publications.
Engage ministries, EdTech hubs, civil society and international partners on evidence-based policy and practice.
requirements: PhD in education technology, machine learning in education, educational data science, curriculum and instruction with technology, or a related field.
Experience conducting high-quality education, technology or AI research in a research institution or similar setting.
Strong quantitative and/or qualitative research skills; mixed-methods experience is an advantage.
Proficiency with relevant analysis software such as STATA, SPSS, R, Python, NVivo or ATLAS.ti.
Excellent scientific writing, proposal writing and English-language communication skills.
benefits: Appointment level may range from postdoctoral to senior research scientist depending on experience.
Internationally Recruited Position based at APHRC headquarters in Nairobi.

title: Climate and Health Research Scientist
organization: African Population and Health Research Center (APHRC)
summary: APHRC is recruiting a Climate and Health Research Scientist in Kenya to lead research on the links between climate change and health across African policy and research contexts.
description: This role sits within APHRC’s Health and Wellbeing Theme. It focuses on research that identifies vulnerability to climate-related health impacts, quantifies and models climate-health risks, and tests adaptation, mitigation and resilience solutions.
source: African Population and Health Research Center (APHRC)
verification_notes: Verified from the APHRC official career page; APHRC lists the May 31, 2026 deadline, so this opportunity should appear in the archive.
responsibilities: Conduct and lead research on climate and health, including vulnerability assessment, impact quantification, modelling and intervention testing.
Lead proposal development, scientific writing and climate-health research strategy.
Provide technical oversight to multidisciplinary teams spanning climate science, health, epidemiology, policy, communications and data science.
Build partnerships with African and global research institutions and support consortium-based research delivery.
Translate evidence into policy insights and engage communities, policymakers and regional bodies.
requirements: PhD in environmental health, environmental science, geography, climate science, epidemiology, public health or a related field.
At least six years of relevant postdoctoral experience in climate-health research.
Strong publication and scientific writing record on climate and health interlinkages.
Experience designing or leading multicountry research programmes and developing grant proposals.
Quantitative skills in climate-health data generation, management, analysis, modelling or attribution science.
benefits: Appointment may be at Research Scientist, Senior Research Scientist or Principal Research Scientist level depending on experience.
Internationally Recruited Position within APHRC’s research division.

title: Short-Term Internship
summary: CERN offers a Short-Term Internship in Geneva for students who need or are encouraged to complete practical training in a technical, engineering or administrative field.
description: The internship gives students practical exposure to CERN teams and real projects connecting science, technology and innovation. Interns support experienced professionals, apply tools and methods from their studies, and work in an international environment.
verification_notes: Verified from CERN official career page.
responsibilities: Support experienced professionals in daily activities.
Learn and apply tools or methods relevant to the intern’s area of study.

requirements: Currently studying in a technical, engineering or administrative field.
Post-secondary education at technical/administrative diploma, bachelor’s or master’s level.
Required or encouraged by the university to complete practical training.
Good knowledge of English or French and continued full-time student enrolment during the internship.
Nationality of a CERN Member State or Associate Member State, subject to CERN’s stated eligibility rules.
benefits: Monthly net allowance of 1,593 Swiss francs.
Contract duration from 1 to 6 months; paid leave for contracts of 4 months or more.

title: Bioinformaticist Research Scientist
summary: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory is recruiting a Bioinformaticist Research Scientist for the Joint Genome Institute. The role supports genomics, computational biology and scientific data analysis for research teams in Berkeley, California.
description: This research scientist role sits at the intersection of bioinformatics, genomics, data analysis and scientific computing. The position supports the Joint Genome Institute by developing and applying computational approaches to biological data, contributing to research workflows, and collaborating with scientists who use genomic information to answer energy, environment and bioscience questions.
verification_notes: Verified from the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory career posting.
responsibilities: Develop and apply bioinformatics workflows for genomic and related biological datasets.
Analyze, interpret and communicate complex sequence and omics data for research teams.
Collaborate with laboratory scientists, computational researchers and project stakeholders at the Joint Genome Institute.
Support reproducible analysis practices, documentation and data-quality checks.
Contribute scientific insight to research outputs, reports, presentations or publications.
requirements: Strong background in bioinformatics, computational biology, genomics or a related scientific field.
Experience analyzing biological sequence or omics datasets using reproducible computational methods.
Programming and data-analysis skills relevant to scientific computing, such as scripting, workflow development and statistical analysis.
Ability to collaborate across multidisciplinary scientific teams and communicate technical results clearly.
Candidates must be eligible to work in the United States at the time of hire.
benefits: Expected salary range is $110,484 - $154,680, with full range dependent on skills and experience.
Work at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and the Joint Genome Institute on nationally significant bioscience research.
Access to a multidisciplinary research environment spanning genomics, computing, energy and environmental science.
